examples.explanation: Bug: display property should be 'grid' instead of 'inline-grid'

hints.text: Check the display property and ensure it is set to 'grid' instead of 'inline-grid'

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout Bugs
editorial.intuition: To fix the bug, we need to analyze the CSS string and identify the issue. In this case, the display property is set to 'inline-grid' instead of 'grid', which prevents the grid from displaying correctly. We can modify the display property to fix the bug.
editorial.bruteForce: One way to fix the bug is to manually analyze the CSS string and modify the display property. However, this approach can be time-consuming and prone to errors.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a CSS parser or a string manipulation library to parse the input string and modify the display property programmatically.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of the solution by testing it with different inputs and verifying that it produces the expected output.
commonMistakes: forgetting to set the display property to 'grid'
using the wrong unit for grid-template-columns
